description_long: This object is a rectangular, compact electronic device with a predominantly gray body and a black faceplate. It features a distinctive cross-shaped directional pad on the left, two round red buttons on the right, and two small rectangular buttons in the center, all set into a slightly recessed surface. The device is reminiscent of classic video game controllers, specifically the Nintendo Entertainment System (NES) controller, with its iconic color scheme and button layout. The casing appears to be made of hard plastic, with a smooth, slightly glossy finish. The object is small enough to be handheld and is likely lightweight, with internal circuitry and possibly a small amount of metal for electrical contacts. There are no visible screens or displays, and the object is designed for manual interaction via its physical buttons.
description: A compact, rectangular gray and black electronic controller with a cross-shaped directional pad and two red buttons, reminiscent of a classic NES game controller. The surface is smooth plastic with a slightly glossy finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full button layout: cross-shaped directional pad on the left, two small rectangular buttons in the center, and two red circular buttons on the right.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the height and thickness of the device, with the two red buttons most prominent.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the controller from the opposite side, with the directional pad now on the right and the red buttons on the left.
description_view_3: Side view focusing on the cross-shaped directional pad, with the red buttons less visible.
